creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Best CHEAP day trip from Copenhagen 🇩🇰👇

Here are a few things you need to know about the day trip from Copenhagen to Hamlet’s Castle in Elsinore:

The trains leave every 15 minutes from Copenhagen Central Station, Nørreport, and Østerport. The journey time is about 55 minutes.

You can purchase tickets on the day in the ticket machines or buy them in advance and get a discount.

Tickets for Kronborg Castle (aka Hamlet’s Castle) cost 125 DKK (around €17) and can be bought at the entrance or online.

The street food market is called Værftets Madmarket (if you want to load it up in Google Maps).

Most importantly, I’ve just uploaded a long video about this day trip to my YT channel, where I’m sharing much more information about the city of Elsinore and the castle. There’s a link in my bio 😇  I had only been to Elsinore and the castle once when I was a kid, so I was pleasantly surprised when I visited the other day - an excellent experience if you want to see a bit more of Denmark on your trip to Copenhagen 🇩🇰

#copenhagen #elsinore #kronborgcastle #denmarktravel

transcription.text: It blows my mind that tourists are paying crazy amounts of money for day trips in Copenhagen. Instead, you should just get the train that leaves every 15 minutes and you'll reach the city center of one of the coziest small Danish towns in just under an hour. Enjoy the town and then head down to the waterfront and visit one of the best preserved castles in Scandinavia. The castle is called Kronborg and this is where Shakespeare's famous Hamlet takes place. When you head down into the dungeons, you can also see one of the most famous legends in Denmark, who will wake up and protect Denmark whenever the country faces grave danger. Afterwards, you can head over to the cozy street food market and get some food at a fraction of the price you'd pay in the city center of Copenhagen.
